message:
fix(ext4): bound block reads to filesystem size

bd-r7hbs

files.patch:
@@ -1118,6 +1118,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-r37m","title":"Implement btrfs punch-hole fallocate support","description":"FEATURE_PARITY and README still document btrfs punch-hole fallocate as unsupported. Implement btrfs fallocate(PUNCH_HOLE|KEEP_SIZE) in ffs-core, convert unit/log/E2E coverage from rejection to success semantics, and update parity/docs accordingly.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-22T17:39:19.651814302Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T18:53:18.953120296Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T18:53:18.952665987Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-r3vpm","title":"[HIGH] ffs-harness rename mismatch helper exceeds clippy too_many_lines","description":"# Review finding

A fresh workspace clippy rerun exposed another red-gate failure in `ffs-harness` test code.

## Failure
- File: `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs:6367`
- Item: `fn assert_rename_file_directory_type_mismatch_contract(mnt: &Path, scenario_id: &str)`
- Lint: `clippy::too_many_lines`
- Current size: 106 lines vs 100-line limit

## Why this matters
This now keeps `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` red even after the existing `ffs-tui` and `ffs-cli` issues are fixed.

## Repro
`rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`

## Expected fix direction
Split the helper into smaller assertion helpers or setup/verify phases so the test contract stays readable without tripping the pedantic line-budget gate.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc-fs","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:26:22.260333888Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T03:46:55.352280849Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T03:46:55.350008409Z","close_reason":"Refactored: assert_rename_file_directory_type_mismatch_contract at crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs:6850 now delegates to two focused sub-helpers (assert_rename_file_over_directory_rejected_with_eisdir and assert_rename_directory_over_file_rejected_with_enotdir). Local #[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)] removed; outer function is ~10 lines. Workspace clippy green for fuse_e2e (exit 0 on cargo clippy -p ffs-harness --test fuse_e2e). All ffs-harness tests still compile via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["clippy","ffs-harness","qa","review","workspace-gate"]}
 {"id":"bd-r7di0","title":"Add metamorphic coverage for inode checksum field-content invariance","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:03:31.039875346Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T21:28:39.221685045Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T21:28:39.219639844Z","close_reason":"Added ffs-core metamorphic target for inode checksum field-content invariance; rch fmt/check/clippy/test passed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-r7hbs","title":"Bound ext4 block reads to declared block count","description":"Ext4ImageReader::read_block currently validates only the backing slice length. If an image file has appended bytes beyond the ext4 volume, malformed metadata can reference blocks >= s_blocks_count and read those bytes as filesystem data. Reject block numbers at or beyond the parsed superblock's blocks_count and add regression coverage with an oversized backing image.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T22:40:39.152477833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T22:48:49.515407146Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T22:48:49.513494523Z","close_reason":"Rejected ext4 block reads at or beyond s_blocks_count even when the backing image has appended bytes; added oversized-image regression coverage. Validated with cargo fmt --check, focused ffs-ondisk test, workspace cargo check, workspace clippy -D warnings, and UBS on ext4.rs.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","parser","reliability"]}
 {"id":"bd-r7od","title":"Add generic/112 preallocation conformance coverage for ext4 and btrfs","description":"Upstream xfstests generic/112 runs fsx with -A and -x to exercise AIO plus preallocated unwritten extents. FrankenFS already has fallocate tests, but the harness lacks an explicit cross-filesystem conformance check for the preallocation contract that generic/112 depends on. Add deterministic ext4+btrfs harness coverage proving that preallocation extends size, unwritten regions read as zeroes, writes into preallocated space preserve surrounding zero-fill, and the contract survives reopen.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T17:23:32.225284126Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T18:00:09.368172019Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T18:00:09.365241100Z","close_reason":"Added generic/112 preallocation conformance coverage for ext4 and btrfs via shared harness assertions","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-r7vx0","title":"[HIGH] restore missing fuzz_ioctl_dispatch corpus dir for crash promotion pipeline","description":"Workspace gate regression from `cargo test --workspace --all-targets` on 2026-04-22 via `rch` worker `vmi1156319`:

- `crash_promotion::tests::corpus_directories_exist` panics because `fuzz/corpus/fuzz_ioctl_dispatch` is missing
- `crash_promotion::tests::pipeline_validation_passes` then fails with `22/23 corpus directories found`

Root cause is repo drift between the fuzz target inventory and the shipped corpus directories. Acceptance:
1. `fuzz/corpus/fuzz_ioctl_dispatch/` exists with at least one seed or the pipeline no longer requires it.
2. `crates/ffs-harness/src/crash_promotion.rs` inventory and repo layout are consistent.
3. `cargo test -p ffs-harness crash_promotion -- --nocapture` passes via `rch`.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:45:08.560673853Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T23:17:39Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T23:17:39Z","close_reason":"Added the missing fuzz_ioctl_dispatch seed corpus and verified crash-promotion pipeline checks via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-r8c8r","title":"[HIGH] fixture checksum manifest drifted for ext4 inline-data continuation JSON","description":"Reproduced from `env AGENT_NAME=cod-fs CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets` on 2026-04-22.

Failure:
- `fixture_checksum_manifest_is_complete` at `crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs:3680`
- fixture: `ext4_inode_inline_data_with_continuation.json`
- manifest digest: `b040217daafc0d5a7706a9263652b33776989f5b7c5c7f76e807a243860f6218`
- actual digest: `5f811167369ea9e1bee12196a8ce72f070cd08105d85473401e22dc28a646525`

The fixture content and checksum manifest are out of sync, so the conformance gate stays red even when parser behavior is otherwise stable.

Acceptance:
- fixture and checksum manifest agree again
- `fixture_checksum_manifest_is_complete` passes
- `full_conformance_gate_pass` is no longer blocked by this manifest slice.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T18:25:18.016914474Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T21:24:27Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T21:24:27Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","ext4","fixtures","review"]}
